Chief Executive Officer
source_jobs.description: <h3 style="text-align: center;"><strong>Chief Executive Officer</strong></h3> <p><strong>Company:</strong> Confidential (Australian Family Office, Home Services Platform)<br><strong>Location:</strong> Sydney, Australia<br><strong>Reports to:</strong> Board / Family Office principals<br><strong>Compensation:</strong> US$350K–$500K base + performance bonus + exceptional equity<br><strong>Search partner:</strong> Sitreps</p> <hr> <h3><strong>About the Client</strong></h3> <p>Our client is the private investment arm of an established Australian family with a multi-billion-dollar balance sheet across real estate, public and private equities, and direct operating investments. The home services platform is a direct investment, owned and capitalized entirely by the family, with the operating freedom that comes from a single committed LP and no forced exit timeline.</p> <p>The family name carries significant weight in the Australian business community and functions as a closing tool with sellers, employees, and capital partners. The full name is shared with qualified candidates under NDA.</p> <h3><strong>About the Role</strong></h3> <p>An Australian family office is building a PE-style home services roll-up in Australia. The Australian residential HVAC and plumbing market is roughly the size of California, with limited PE-backed competition in country and no operator holding meaningful market share. ServiceTitan and Nexstar only entered the market in the last 1–2 years. Less than 10% of operators use a modern field service platform, and almost none run fixed-pricing or sales-culture playbooks.</p> <p>Put plainly: this looks like the US residential services market did before the major roll-ups consolidated it. Same fragmented base, same legacy ownership, same uplift available to anyone with the playbook and the capital to execute. The difference is there's no race; our client is the first serious well-capitalized entrant.</p> <p>The firm has approved the strategy, has an active deal pipeline in motion, and is moving multiple acquisitions toward diligence in the near term. The capital is the family's own balance sheet, deployed deal-by-deal with no external LPs and no forced exit clock. The plan models $300–450M AUD in revenue and $60–70M EBITDA over 5 years; we'd build past that if the runway is there.</p> <p>We're hiring the CEO to lead this platform from greenfield to category leader.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <p><strong>The platform.</strong> P&amp;L, capital allocation, M&amp;A pacing, board interface with the family principals. You set the operating cadence and the cultural standard for the holding company.</p> <p><strong>The playbook.</strong> The leading US roll-ups have spent close to a decade refining theirs. We're at the equivalent of year 2. You will build, not inherit. ServiceTitan rollouts, dispatch optimization, fixed pricing, technician comp design, training programs, integration sequencing, this is the work.</p> <p><strong>The team.</strong> Hire the COO (if not already in seat alongside you), the Market Area Presidents, finance, integration, marketing. The platform leadership layer is yours to build.</p> <p><strong>The deal book.</strong> Partner with the deal team on target selection, valuation discipline, and seller relationships. Several pipeline sellers have said they will only close if a credible US operator is attached to the platform; the CEO seat is, in part, that operator.</p> <p><strong>The narrative.</strong> Be the face of the platform to sellers, employees, technicians, ca

source_jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
source_jobs.description: <h3 style="text-align: center;"><strong>Chief Operating Officer</strong></h3> <p><strong>Company:</strong> Confidential (Australian Family Office, Home Services Platform)<br><strong>Location:</strong> Sydney, Australia<br><strong>Reports to:</strong> CEO (Platform)<br><strong>Compensation:</strong> US$300K–$450K base + performance bonus + exceptional equity<br><strong>Search partner:</strong> Sitreps</p> <hr> <h3><strong>About the Client</strong></h3> <p>Our client is the private investment arm of an established Australian family with a multi-billion-dollar balance sheet across real estate, public and private equities, and direct operating investments. The home services platform is a direct investment, owned and capitalized entirely by the family, with the operating freedom that comes from a single committed LP and no forced exit timeline.</p> <p>The family name carries significant weight in the Australian business community. The full name is shared with qualified candidates under NDA.</p> <h3><strong>About the Role</strong></h3> <p>An Australian family office is building a PE-style home services roll-up in Australia. The Australian residential HVAC and plumbing market is roughly the size of California, with limited PE-backed competition in country and no operator holding meaningful market share. ServiceTitan and Nexstar only entered the market in the last 1–2 years. Less than 10% of operators run modern field service software or fixed-pricing sales cultures.</p> <p>This looks like the US residential services market did before the major roll-ups consolidated it: fragmented base, legacy ownership, real uplift available to anyone with the playbook and the capital to execute. Our client is the first serious well-capitalized entrant. The deal pipeline is live, with multiple acquisitions moving toward diligence in the near term; the platform plan models $300–450M AUD in revenue and $60–70M EBITDA over 5 years, against a market that's still wide open.</p> <p>We're hiring the COO to run the operating engine while the CEO leads strategy, capital, and the board. If you've spent the last several years inside a PE-backed home services platform watching how the playbook actually gets built and run, this is the seat where you stop watching and start building it yourself, from the ground up, in a new country.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Operations across the platform.</strong> Same-branch performance, dispatch productivity, technician utilization, sell rates, gross margin, working capital. Every operating KPI lives with you.</li> <li><strong>The Market Area Presidents.</strong> Direct line management of the regional / market-level operating leaders. You set their cadence, their targets, and their development paths.</li> <li><strong>The integration engine.</strong> Each acquired company arrives with a founder-GM, legacy systems, and a local culture. Standing up the integration playbook (ServiceTitan, fixed pricing, sales training, financial reporting, branded marketing) is core COO work.</li> <li><strong>The playbook itself.</strong> Our client is at the operating maturity equivalent of a major US consolidator at year 2–3. You will write SOPs, training curricula, comp plans, and ops dashboards that don't exist yet. This is build mode, not run mode.</li> <li><strong>Talent and culture.</strong> Help the CEO hire the platform leadership layer and the next generation of MAPs and GMs. Set the operating cultural standard for what "good" looks like at scale.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>R

source_jobs.title: Country Operations Manager - Ukraine
Country Sales Manager
source_jobs.description: <h3 style="text-align: center;"><strong>Country Sales Manager — USA Remote · USA-Based Candidates Only</strong></h3> <p><strong>Compensation:</strong> $170k Base + Uncapped Commission (Top Performers Earn $300k+)</p> <hr> <h3><strong>About the Client</strong></h3> <p>An established, Asia-headquartered global leader in high-precision motion systems used inside the equipment that builds and tests semiconductors. With decades of engineering excellence and a roster of blue-chip semiconductor customers, the company is now expanding its presence in the North American market.</p> <h3><strong>About the Role</strong></h3> <p>This is a ground-floor opportunity to build and lead the company's U.S. commercial operations — with the full backing of an established global engineering organization and a dedicated U.S.-based Sales Engineer already onboard to partner with you on the technical side of the business.</p> <h3><strong>How to Think About the Role</strong></h3> <p>Think of a hamburger. The fabs (Intel, TSMC, Samsung, Micron) are the customer eating the cheeseburger. The big OEMs and machine builders (the back-end semiconductor capital equipment companies) are the ones making and selling the cheeseburger to those customers. Our company makes the patty — the motion subsystem that goes inside their machines.</p> <p>We do not sell directly to the fabs. We sell to the people building the machines that go into those fabs, and we sell through the channel partners and distributors who supply them.</p> <p>The right person for this role understands how to sell components and subsystems into OEMs — not how to sell finished tools to fab customers. They've sold patties, not cheeseburgers.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Develop and execute the company's go-to-market strategy for the U.S. back-end semiconductor capital equipment market</li> <li>Build and manage a pipeline of high-value OEM accounts — back-end machine builders, packaging and assembly equipment makers, test and inspection companies</li> <li>Establish and grow relationships with engineering teams, procurement leaders, and design-in decision-makers at target OEMs</li> <li>Build and manage a channel partner and distributor network across the U.S.</li> <li>Work closely with the company’s overseas engineering and product teams to bring customer requirements into product specifications</li> <li>Partner directly with the U.S.-based Sales Engineer to convert technical opportunities into closed business — they own pre-sales engineering, demos, and technical specs while you own commercial strategy and customer relationships</li> <li>Oversee sales forecasting, budgeting, and reporting to senior management</li> <li>Manage long design-in sales cycles (typically 9–18 months) from first contact to closed purchase order</li> <li>Provide regular competitive and market intelligence back to the global team</li> <li>Lay the foundation for a broader U.S. sales team as the business scales</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Requirements</strong></h3> <h3><strong>Must-Haves</strong></h3> <ul> <li>5+ years selling into back-end semiconductor manufacturing equipment OEMs — this is non-negotiable. Strong candidates will come from either: <ul> <li>Motion control / precision components companies selling into back-end semi OEMs — Aerotech, PI (Physik Instrumente), Parker Hannifin, Dover Motion, Kollmorgen, Yaskawa, Beckhoff, Celera Motion, Renishaw, or similar</li> <li>Back-end semiconductor OEMs themselves — ASM Pacific Technology, Kulicke &amp; Soffa, Besi,

Engagement Manager - Air Force Accounts
source_jobs.description: <h3 style="text-align: center;"><strong>Engagement Manager - Air Force Accounts</strong></h3> <p><strong>Location:</strong> Arlington, VA (In-office, with up to 50% travel)<br><strong>Compensation:</strong> $200k-$230k + 30% bonus + Equity</p> <hr> <h3><strong>About the Role</strong></h3> <p>We are one of the fastest growing defense technology companies, with top-tier VC backing. We are looking for dynamic Engagement Managers to lead client delivery projects, driving adoption of a SaaS platform and advisory services for national security clients. This role involves managing senior client relationships, executing high-impact projects, and fostering account growth through strategic problem-solving and data-driven solutions.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Lead project execution, ensuring delivery exceeds client expectations while optimizing team resources.</li> <li>Develop engagement plans, align resources, and drive adoption of SaaS solutions tailored to client workflows.</li> <li>Manage senior-level client relationships, translating needs into actionable software use cases.</li> <li>Collaborate with product and sales teams to scope new work and identify expansion opportunities.</li> <li>Mentor analysts, provide quality assurance, and present insights to executives concisely.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Requirements</strong></h3> <h3><strong>Must-Haves</strong></h3> <ul> <li>4+ years in client engagement or consulting, with expertise in national security strategy, supply chain, federal procurement, or related fields.</li> <li>Strong business acumen, exceptional communication, and ability to manage complex projects independently.</li> <li>U.S. citizenship required; current or ability to obtain a security clearance.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Nice-to-Haves</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Familiarity with SaaS, data analytics, or advisory services; experience in government or defense sectors preferred.</li> <li>MBA or advanced degree from a top-tier institution is preferred.</li> </ul>

source_jobs.description: <h3 style="text-align: center;"><strong>Engagement Manager</strong></h3> <p><strong>Location:</strong> Arlington, VA (In-office, with up to 50% travel. Travel will be to Europe, INDOPACOM 50%+)<br><strong>Compensation:</strong> $180-200k + 100% potential bonus + equity</p> <hr> <h3><strong>About the Role</strong></h3> <p>We are one of the fastest growing defense technology companies, with top-tier VC backing. We are looking for dynamic Engagement Managers to lead client delivery projects, driving adoption of a SaaS platform and advisory services for national security clients. This role involves managing senior client relationships, executing high-impact projects, and fostering account growth through strategic problem-solving and data-driven solutions.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Lead project execution, ensuring delivery exceeds client expectations while optimizing team resources.</li> <li>Develop engagement plans, align resources, and drive adoption of SaaS solutions tailored to client workflows.</li> <li>Manage senior-level client relationships, translating needs into actionable software use cases.</li> <li>Collaborate with product and sales teams to scope new work and identify expansion opportunities.</li> <li>Mentor analysts, provide quality assurance, and present insights to executives concisely.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Requirements</strong></h3> <h3><strong>Must-Haves</strong></h3> <ul> <li>2-5 years in client engagement or consulting, with expertise in national security strategy, supply chain, federal procurement, or defense tech engagement.</li> <li>Strong business acumen, exceptional communication, and ability to manage complex projects independently.</li> <li>U.S. citizenship required; current or ability to obtain a security clearance.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Nice-to-Haves</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Familiarity with SaaS, data analytics, or advisory services; experience in government or defense sectors preferred.</li> <li>MBA or advanced degree from a top-tier institution is preferred.</li> </ul>

Forward Deployed Data Scientist
source_jobs.description: <h3 style="text-align: center;"><strong>Forward Deployed Data Scientist</strong></h3> <p><strong>Location:</strong> Arlington, VA; Pittsburgh, PA; or Remote</p> <p><strong>Travel:</strong> Up to 75%</p> <p><strong>Role Type:</strong> Full-time<br>$140-$175K + up to 30% bonus + Equity</p> <hr> <h3><strong>About the Client</strong></h3> <p>We are a defense technology firm focused on national security. Our AI-enabled platform supports the defense acquisition lifecycle, from supply chain and logistics to modernization and sustainment. By replacing outdated manual processes with high-fidelity data and advanced analytics, we empower the national security community and every branch of the military to make faster, more informed decisions for the future of defense.</p> <h3><strong>About the Role</strong></h3> <p>We are seeking an inquisitive <strong>Forward Deployed Data Scientist</strong> with deep expertise in Artificial Intelligence and a sophisticated understanding of complex Defense Acquisition challenges. You will work with proprietary and commercial datasets to uncover critical connections, derive actionable knowledge, and identify potential risks that help government leaders make mission-critical decisions.</p> <p>Your role involves designing, developing, and rapidly deploying scalable AI solutions that transform data into strategic capabilities. You must be an AI expert with a strong command of data science fundamentals and a proven ability to bring data-driven solutions to life within the defense ecosystem.</p> <p>The ideal candidate is a highly organized problem-solver and a "scrappy" out-of-the-box thinker. You lead by influence, possess an intolerance for mediocrity, and are motivated to roll up your sleeves to produce simple, impactful solutions to complex problems.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <ul> <li><p><strong>AI Innovation:</strong> Develop and deploy new AI methods and systems to solve complex defense challenges.</p></li> <li><p><strong>Rapid Prototyping:</strong> Build AI models, heuristics, and agentic solutions from the ground up.</p></li> <li><p><strong>Client Engagement:</strong> Work directly with customers to translate vague mission needs into concrete AI workflows and technical requirements.</p></li> <li><p><strong>End-to-End Ownership:</strong> Take full accountability for client problems, from initial discovery to deploying production code into AI systems.</p></li> <li><p><strong>Cross-Functional Collaboration:</strong> Partner with AI platform and data engineering teams to build end-to-end production solutions.</p></li> <li><p><strong>Generalization:</strong> Wrap specific Data Science solutions into reusable agentic AI frameworks and features for broader deployment.</p></li> <li><p><strong>Agentic Systems:</strong> Create agentic AI systems using AI orchestrators and advanced reasoning capabilities.</p></li> </ul> <h3><strong>Requirements</strong></h3> <h3><strong>Must-Haves</strong></h3> <ul> <li><p>U.S. Citizenship is required.</p></li> <li><p><strong>Technical Translation:</strong> Proven ability to embed with clients, understand vague national security challenges, and translate them into concrete technical documentation for multiple teams.</p></li> <li><p><strong>Prototyping &amp; Deployment:</strong> Expertise in rapidly prototy

General Manager - Home Healthcare (Las Vegas, NV)
source_jobs.description: <h3 style="text-align: center;"><strong>General Manager - Home Healthcare</strong></h3> <p><strong>Market-Level P&amp;L Leadership | Full-Time | On-Site / Hybrid</strong></p> <hr> <p><strong>Location: </strong>Las Vegas, NV</p> <p><strong>Industry: </strong>AI-Native Post Acute Care</p> <p><strong>Compensation: </strong>$150-$200 + Bonus + Equity</p> <h3><strong>About the Client</strong></h3> <p>We are fixing US healthcare by building an AI-native physical care platform, starting with home health. Home health is a $140B industry generating less than $10B in EBITDA — roughly 40% of revenue is consumed by pure administrative waste. We automate that waste with AI, creating a fundamentally different cost structure that lets us rapidly take market share by serving the 30% of patients who go untreated today.</p> <p>Every dollar of revenue we generate removes two dollars from the healthcare system. If we succeed, so does US healthcare.</p> <p>Our platform operates at materially higher gross margins than traditional home health providers. We pay clinicians more, strip out their admin burden, and unlock scale economics that have eluded this fragmented industry for decades. We are backed by world-class venture investors and scaling nationally through organic growth and acquisition.</p> <h3><strong>About the Role</strong></h3> <p>We are hiring General Managers to own and operate individual markets as we scale nationally. This is a P&amp;L ownership role. You will run your market — a metropolitan area or MSA — as your own business. You own the census, the margin, the team, the clinical quality, and the growth trajectory.</p> <p>Some GMs will take over existing operations and transform them. Others will stand up new markets from scratch, including post-acquisition integrations. All will operate with the intensity, ownership, and judgment of a founder running a business backed by a growth-stage platform and top-tier investors. All GMs receive a robust onboarding program to learn the industry and the business before stepping into the seat.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <h3><strong>Market P&amp;L</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Full accountability for revenue, census growth, gross margin, and unit economics</li> <li>Drive census growth in partnership with Sales — translate referral pipeline into admitted, scheduled, and served patients</li> <li>Leverage data and AI-driven tooling for daily resource allocation decisions that convert top-line growth into bottom-line results</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Operations</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Run daily execution across scheduling, staffing, clinical compliance, and service delivery</li> <li>Single point of accountability for compliance, survey readiness, and clinical quality in your market</li> <li>Partner cross-functionally with centralized Technology, Compliance, and Operations — you own local outcomes</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Team</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Hire, develop, and lead your market's operations team — Schedulers, Patient Care Coordinators, Clinical Managers, Sales Reps, and field clinicians</li> <li>Build bench strength ahead of volume; sequence hiring and training to support growth, not just maintain current operations</li> <li>Set clear expectations, coach through structured routines, and make the hard calls on performance</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Platform</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Serve as the critical feedback loop between field operations and our AI/product teams&lt
